response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of an open-source Geo-Data/GIS platform used in production, GIS web applications since 2013) and proven low-latency systems work (DLang microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, with async I/O, fibers, and caching). However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ or Rust, and the candidate's C++ exposure is limited and dated (a 7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a bachelor thesis), with recent years spent mostly in TypeScript/Node.js product work. DLang experience is systems-adjacent and partially transferable, but there is no direct routing/navigation pipeline experience, making this a domain-adjacent rather than direct fit.
strengths: Strong geospatial domain background: founded and operates an open-source GIS/map-data platform used by Green Map
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of requests/sec) using async I/O and caching in DLang on Linux
Broad full-stack versatility, strong testing culture (TDD, CI/CD), and demonstrated willingness to learn new stacks
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and dated (2014-2015); no Rust experience listed
No direct experience with routing engines, routing tiles, or navigation guidance systems
Recent roles (2020-present) are predominantly TypeScript/Node.js product work, distant from systems-level pipeline engineering
